page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.24xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration delivers 96 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 3.3 GHz. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance features 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps, facilitating data transfer between the instance and external storage volumes or other network resources.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers high memory density and dedicated CPU resources, making it suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that require substantial RAM without the overhead of shared-core architectures. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators represents a tradeoff, requiring external network storage configurations and limiting its suitability for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning workloads. It is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, and enterprise resource planning systems.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.24xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, it features 96 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core running at 3.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large databases, and workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without hyperthreading. The lack of local storage and GPUs represents a key tradeoff, positioning the instance specifically for memory-dense, CPU-bound tasks that utilize network storage.
